scenarios.description: User wants a prompt to classify customer feedback
scenarios.query: Write a prompt that classifies customer feedback as positive, negative, or neutral
expected_behavior: Starts with zero-shot baseline on the escalation ladder
Output format constraint is explicit (returns one of three labels)
Provides one happy-path and one edge-case eval scenario
Does NOT jump to few-shot without evaluating zero-shot first

scenarios.description: User needs a prompt that answers questions from a document
scenarios.query: Write a prompt that answers questions about an uploaded PDF
expected_behavior: Includes hallucination-reduction instruction ('based only on provided context')
Includes 'if not in context, say I don't know'
Uses CoT or chaining for multi-step extraction if document is long
Does NOT rely solely on Claude's parametric knowledge

scenarios.description: User has an existing prompt with excessive CRITICAL/ALWAYS overuse
scenarios.query: Improve this prompt: ALWAYS CRITICAL: NEVER do X. IMPORTANT: YOU MUST do Y. REMEMBER: ALWAYS...
expected_behavior: Rewrites instructions in normal, clear prose
Output prompt is shorter and clearer than the original
